A.rationale: Correct chunking, ValueError on n<=0, empty-list natural, true iterator caveat; full Args/Returns docstring. Slightly padded with key-decisions narration.

B.rationale: Same correct code; smoke test adds real verification value, but longest answer with extra open-question prose. Signal-per-token lowest of the four.

C.rationale: Tightest answer; adds isinstance(n,int) check matching 'positive integer' claim, clean docstring, minimal accurate edge notes. Best signal-per-token, no fluff.

D.rationale: Correct, concise failure-mode notes, true iterator caveat. Uses {n} not {n!r}, slightly less precise message for string inputs; otherwise solid.

notes: All share the same correct core. C wins on conciseness plus a stronger integer-type check; B's smoke test is useful but it is the most verbose. A and D are solid middles.
